result.short_description: payment-processor audit log writes failing — CloudSQL disk at 95% capacity in prod-uk
result.description: payment-processor audit log inserts have been failing intermittently since 16:08 UTC. The underlying CloudSQL Postgres instance for the audit trail is at 95% disk capacity and write IO is being throttled. PCI compliance requires every payment authorisation to have a corresponding audit record, so dropped writes are a P2 PCI risk regardless of customer impact. No customer-visible payment failures yet.

result.short_description: loyalty-service audit trail missing for 3,200 point redemption transactions
result.description: Reconciliation job at 14:00 UTC detected 3,200 loyalty-point redemption transactions that have no corresponding audit-trail entry between 08:15 and 09:42. Customer-facing impact: none visible. Internal impact: audit gap means we cannot prove the redemptions were authorised, which fails our annual SOC2 review if not remediated. Cause appears to be the audit-publisher consumer falling silently behind during the Kafka rebalance event reported in INC0042569.

result.short_description: pricing-engine prod-uk showing weekend promotional prices on Monday morning
result.description: Multiple customer reports and one tabloid mention that pricing-engine is serving Saturday-Sunday promotional prices into Monday morning. The price-rollback CronJob that runs at 00:05 UTC every Monday failed silently at 00:05 today — the job pod terminated before the rollback completed. ~14,000 SKUs are affected, mostly outdoor furniture and BBQs. Customer trust impact is the primary concern; estimated direct margin loss is approximately £18,000/hour.
